sourceOfTruth.evidenceBoundary: Read-only service/config/code inspection. No live files, systemd units, env files, callbacks, channels, or services were modified.

userAuthorization.claudePreference: User asked to avoid Claude first; this preflight was performed by Codex directly.

httpGatewayIngress.interpretation: This HTTP/WebSocket gateway ingress is present, but it is not the primary Feishu event consumer for the current app because the Feishu channel uses websocket mode inside the OpenClaw process.

runtimeEvidence: channels.feishu.connectionMode is websocket in both old and shadow configs.
feishuRuntimeMode.interpretation: The cutover must disconnect old OpenClaw's Feishu websocket consumer before starting the shadow consumer for the same app.

evidence: openclaw-benben README states local MJS modules only, dry-run Feishu adapter only, no production ingress, no Feishu send, no model call by default, and no runtime commit.
shadow openclaw.json only references the workspace and local-lite-lane plugin; no openclaw-benben dispatch plugin or runtime hook was found.
service-entry harness method openclaw-benben.shadow.syntheticTurn refuses production ingress, delivery send, provider/model-call enablement, raw output flags, and runtime commits.

repairGateChecklist.changeTarget: Move the existing production Feishu benben app from old openclaw-gateway to openclaw-benben.
repairGateChecklist.changeReason: User wants to keep the existing Feishu app/robot and replace the backend OpenClaw service.
risk: Old and shadow use the same Feishu app credentials; enabling both would create duplicate or competing websocket consumers.
Disabling old Feishu requires adjusting old config guard required channels before restart.
The current shadow service has not been proven to route real Feishu turns through the vNext identity/privacy/memory/tool-gate kernels.
A blind cutover would likely produce a copied old-gateway behavior rather than the intended vNext benben behavior.
rollbackPlan: Keep old openclaw-gateway files and service installed as rollback anchor.
If old Feishu is parked later, restore the old config/env/drop-in snapshots and restart openclaw-gateway.
If shadow Feishu is enabled later and fails canary, disable shadow Feishu, restart openclaw-benben, then restore old Feishu and restart openclaw-gateway.
Do not delete shadow artifacts during rollback.
verificationPlan: Pre-change: prove real production Feishu turns route through vNext dispatch code, not only the generic OpenClaw gateway.
During cutover: confirm old Feishu websocket consumer is disconnected before shadow consumer starts.
Post-cutover: owner DM /status, persona identity, normal answer path, memory admin confirmation, non-owner denial, and high-risk tool gate behavior.
repairGateChecklist.authorizationStatus: User authorized entering repair stage, but execution is blocked by current preflight evidence.

decision.reason: Cutover would not yet satisfy the product requirement that real Feishu turns use the new openclaw-benben vNext runtime core.

recommendedNextWork: Implement a production-facing openclaw-benben dispatch integration, likely as a high-priority before_dispatch plugin loaded only by the shadow service.
The plugin must handle real Feishu turns through the vNext identity kernel, privacy kernel, Memory V4 wrapper, usage ledger, and tool gate.
Keep provider/model execution and Feishu delivery behavior explicitly designed and tested before enabling production ingress.
Add tests proving production Feishu-shaped events cannot bypass identity, privacy, memory truth, and tool gates.
Only after that, rerun this L3 preflight and perform the single-consumer cutover.
